marketState: neutral-to-slightly bearish, oversold near-term technical setup
summary: Henry Hub front-month is trading at $2.894, essentially in line with the model's fair value ($2.917, band $2.42-$3.42) and only 1-4% under fair value out to plus-21 days - a modest bullish tilt but not a strong statistical edge (status reads FAIR at every horizon). Seasonality data (vsMedian, probHigher 1-4w, typicalMove4w, reliability) is entirely null in this snapshot, removing our top-priority input, so longer-horizon conviction is capped. Near-term weather shows cooling demand running +12.8% above the 5-year norm (rangePos 0.75) while the 8-16 day outlook is only marginally above normal (+1.8%, rangePos 0.56) and 24h demand is already fading (-3.3), a modest but fading supportive driver. Fundamentally the picture skews bearish: storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise this week, and managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts, only the 35th percentile of the 5y range, so not an extreme crowded short). Technicals are uniformly bearish (bearish trend and a 50<200 EMA death cross on all six timeframes from 5m to 1w) but 4h/1h RSI (21.8/29.1) and a 1d close below the lower Bollinger band point to an oversold, bounce-prone tape rather than confirmation of a fresh breakdown.

longTerm.rationale: The seasonality module (vsMedian, probHigher, typicalMove4w, reliability) is completely empty, so the highest-priority long-term input is unavailable; remaining fundamentals conflict - a +184 Bcf storage surplus with a bearish build surprise and a net-short (but not extreme, 35th percentile) COT position argue for caution, while price trading only marginally under a wide fair-value band ($2.42-$3.42) offers no clear directional edge.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for seasonality vsMedian/probHigher/typicalMove data to populate, and for either a decisive close outside the $2.42-$3.42 fair-value band or two consecutive storage reports confirming the surplus is narrowing/widening vs the 5y average before sizing a directional position.

shortTerm.entry: 2.93-2.97 (bounce into the fair-value/round-number zone; avoid chasing the current oversold print)
shortTerm.rationale: All six technical timeframes (5m-1w) show a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA death cross, the futures curve shows the front strip (Aug/Sep/Oct) softer than deferred winter months (no near-term supply squeeze), and storage/COT both lean bearish; however 4h/1h RSI (21.8/29.1) and the 1d close below the lower Bollinger band signal an oversold tape, so the trade waits for a retracement toward fair value ($2.93-2.97) rather than shorting the current low.
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close back above $3.05 (above the recent range and the fair-value midpoint) with RSI reclaiming 50 on the 4h/1d would negate the short thesis and argue for standing aside or flipping bullish.
